figures.caption: Fig. 3: Visualization of the relevance score for open-vocabulary queries, with the ground-truth segmentation of the target object shown in red.

Fig. 2: Details of our neural regularization. A shared conditional MLP takes Gaussian attributes and a granularity condition g as input, and is trained with a variance-weighted loss to regularize the noisy lifted semantics.
Fig. 4: Comparison in 3D open-vocabulary localization on the LERF dataset [18]. Our method only takes a few minutes to achieve the most accurate performance.
